title: notholaena sulphurea var. accessita
intro: Notholaena sulphurea var. accessita, also known as the Sulphur Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Lomariopsidaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the Sulphur Fern has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin irritations, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of the Sulphur Fern, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used to create teas and tonics that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
